cards.front: What is Selection Sort in terms of time complexity?
cards.back: O(n^2) in all cases(n is the number of items being sorted)

cards.front: What is Bubble Sort in terms of space complexity?
cards.back: O(1) as it does not use any extra space

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort for already sorted or reverse sorted arrays?
cards.back: O(n) as it only needs to find the smallest (or largest) element and swap it with the first (or last) element

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort for already sorted arrays?
cards.back: O(n) in the best case scenario, but O(n^2) in the worst case

cards.front: What are the advantages of Bubble Sort over Selection Sort?
cards.back: Bubble Sort is simpler to implement and has less overhead due to fewer comparisons, however Selection Sort has a better average-case time complexity of O(n^2) compared to O(n^2) for Bubble Sort
